div.p: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have given and by this my present charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks of Rievaulx serving God there, for the souls of my father and my mother, and of King David my grandfather, and for my own soul, and of all my brothers and sisters, my ancestors and successors, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, the enitre land of Blainslie, by the boundaries named in the charter of Richard de Morville, for ploughing and cultivating just as it shall seem best and most useful to them, without performing assart, and permission for constructing their own buildings wherever they wish on the burn which is between Blainslie and Milsie, and the entire land of Milsie which Richard de Morville and his heir gave to them, and made firm by their charters, by the same boundaries which are contained in their charters, and the chapel of Park just as it is contained in their charters, and the building at Colmslie and at Buckholm and at Whitelee, just as they were at the feast of Saint Michael next after the first coronation of Richard, son of Henry king of England, provided no enlargement or more buildings are built there than were theere at that time unless by my permission. And so that none of my foresters give or sell wood within their boundaries specified in my charter, I give and grant all these things to them to possess in everlasting tenure, and by this my charter make firm in free and everlasting alm, and I will and instruct that they have and possess all things mentioned above as freely and quitly, fully and honourably, as any alms can most freely and quitly, fully and honourably be had and possessed by monks of the Cistercian order. As witnesses, Jocelin bishop of Glasgow, Arnold abbot of Rievaulx, Hugh my chancellor, Robert my chaplain, Philip de Valognes, Robert of London, William of Lindsay, Malcolm son of Earl Duncan, William Comyn, Gervase Avenel, William de la Hay, Adam son of Gilbert, Adam of Ceres, Adam of Stanford, Herbert the marischal. At Traqhuair.
